user.title: Home Assistant MCP Server
user.description: This runs the full ha-mcp server inside Home Assistant and exposes it remotely through a Home Assistant webhook (reachable via Nabu Casa or any reverse proxy). Select **Submit** to start it; you can change the port, binding, and authentication afterward in the integration options.

abort.already_configured: The Home Assistant MCP Server is already set up.

init.title: Home Assistant MCP Server
init.description:
Configure the in-process MCP server. Changes are applied on save.

{connect_url}

data.server_url: Home Assistant URL for the server (advanced)

data_description.channel: stable: the pinned, tested release. dev: the latest development build, reinstalled on every reload or Home Assistant restart so it stays current. An explicit ha-mcp package below overrides this.
data_description.server_port: Local TCP port the in-process server listens on. Defaults to 9584 so it can coexist with the MCP Server add-on.
data_description.bind_host: 127.0.0.1 keeps the server loopback-only (remote access is via the webhook). 0.0.0.0 additionally allows direct access from your LAN.
data_description.webhook_auth: none: the secret webhook URL is the credential (default). ha_auth: clients sign in with your Home Assistant account.
data_description.pip_spec: Pip requirement installed at runtime. Leave as the default unless testing a pre-release (for example a GitHub tarball URL); an explicit value overrides the release channel.
data_description.server_url: How the in-process server reaches Home Assistant. The loopback default works for almost everyone.

server_start_failed.title: The Home Assistant MCP Server failed to start

server_package_install_failed.title: The Home Assistant MCP Server package could not be installed
